passage.title: Take-Home Bag Trial
passage.text: Our reading team tested two ways to carry take-home papers last month. Sora used an open paper file, but Theo used a snap bag. After one wet week, Theo's papers came home in better shape because the snap bag protected the edges. The team checked each folder and wrote the results in a notebook. When Sora saw the difference, she changed to a snap bag. By Friday, her work came back without bent corners. The students talked about the test before choosing a better method. The group learned that simple storage choices can make school work easier to keep neat.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mostly about?
choices: Finding a bag that protects homework on rainy days
questions.rationale: The whole passage follows one test and what the group learned from it.

questions.prompt: Which kind of holder did Theo use?
questions.rationale: The second sentence directly tells which tool or place was used.

questions.prompt: What did the group check or write down?
questions.rationale: The fourth sentence explains what the group checked or recorded.

questions.prompt: What does difference mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: A loud sound in a room
The way two things are not the same
questions.rationale: Difference means the two results did not match each other.

questions.prompt: What does folder mean in the second sentence?
choices: A person who visits a club
A cover that keeps papers together
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from how it is used in the passage.

questions.prompt: Why did Sora switch to a snap bag?
choices: The first tool was missing from the class
She thought it would stop the pages from bending
The teacher told the student to stop working
The group wanted to decorate the room more
questions.rationale: The student changed after seeing which method worked better.
